Description: Writing complex window-functional CTE-heavy queries that compile efficiently and minimize cloud data warehouse costs.
Building modularizing documenting and testing models within tools like dbt—the backbone of analytics engineering.
Writing clean modular Python (using Pandas or Polars) to handle tasks SQL cannot like complex string parsing or calling APIs.
Managing code repositories branching strategies code reviews and pull requests to maintain a single source of truth.
Designing star schemas understanding facts vs dimensions and handling slowly changing dimensions (SCDs) for clean reporting.
Knowing when to deploy Data Vault for historical tracking or One Big Table (OBT) to maximize performance in columnar databases.
Defining metrics dimensions and caching logic at the warehouse level (e.g. dbt Semantic Layer Cube) for downstream tool consistency.
Structuring databases schemas compute clusters and access controls in platforms like Snowflake BigQuery or Databricks.
Understanding how data lands via tools like Fivetran/Airbyte and managing raw unstructured or semi-structured data (JSON/Parquet).
Setting up automated testing and deployment workflows (e.g. GitHub Actions) so code changes do not break production.
Implementing assertion testing (schema uniqueness null values) and monitoring pipeline health with tools like Monte Carlo.
Profiling query execution plans cluster scaling laws and storage partitioning to keep cloud warehouse bills under control.
Setting up business intelligence environments (Looker Tableau Power BI) managing connections permissions and extracts cleanly.
Structuring underlying warehouse tables (materialized views aggregate tables) specifically to ensure BI dashboards load instantly.
Pushing transformed data out of the warehouse back into operational tools (Salesforce HubSpot Zendesk) using Hightouch or Census.
Designing clean intuitive visual hierarchies in dashboards so business stakeholders can find answers without training.
Implementing data dictionaries column-level lineage tracking and tag-based masking rules to ensure compliance (GDPR/CCPA).
Applying software engineering practices (sprints documentation-as-code technical debt management) to data team workflows.
Serving as the bridge between raw engineering constraints and ambiguous business questions to define clear data product requirements.
